macro_summary: Markets open this Monday returning from the Juneteenth holiday into a complex backdrop: the U.S. and Iran signed a 14-point MOU on June 19 in Geneva, reopening the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days and sending oil prices to three-month lows (~$80-$83/bbl), but the Fed's first meeting under Chair Kevin Warsh delivered a hawkish shock — 9 of 18 FOMC members now project at least one rate hike in 2026, with markets pricing in a 25bp hike as early as October. The week ahead is the most catalytically dense in the near term, with FedEx (June 23) and Micron (June 24) earnings, followed by May PCE and Q1 GDP final estimate on June 25 — data that will directly test whether the Fed's upgraded 3.6% PCE inflation forecast is conservative or aggressive. Today itself is light on scheduled data, per published calendars, making the week's upcoming prints the dominant risk events.
key_events: Fed held rates at 3.50%-3.75% (June 17); 9/18 dot-plot members see ≥1 hike in 2026; median year-end rate now 3.8%, up from 3.4% in March
Markets now price a 25bp hike by October 2026 per CME FedWatch
U.S.-Iran MOU signed June 19 in Geneva; Strait of Hormuz to reopen within 30 days; WTI crude fell to ~$80/bbl, a 3-month low
Intel surged +10.6% on June 18 after Trump posted Apple-Intel U.S. chip deal; neither company confirmed
Housing starts collapsed 15.4% in May to 1.177M SAAR vs. ~1.445M estimate; NAHB index at 35
30-yr mortgage rate: 6.47% (Freddie Mac, June 18), down from 6.52% prior week but elevated vs. pre-Iran-war ~6%
Key week ahead: FedEx Q4 earnings June 23, Micron Q3 earnings June 24, May PCE + Q1 GDP final estimate June 25, Fed bank stress test results June 24

ideas.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Micron reports fiscal Q3 2026 earnings after the close on June 24 — the most anticipated semiconductor print of the quarter. The setup is exceptional: Q2 saw revenues up 196% YoY with record 75% adjusted gross margins and EPS beating consensus by 33%, HBM capacity is fully sold out through 2026 under binding contracts, and multiple top-tier banks (RBC to $1,200, Stifel to $1,500, Wedbush to $1,300, Aletheia to $1,600) have raised targets dramatically in the past two weeks. Consensus for Q3 is $19.95 EPS on $34.66B revenue, with Q4 guidance above $39.6B being the key upside trigger.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings report on June 24, 2026 after market close, with analyst post-earnings call at 6:00 PM EDT; the Q4 revenue guidance print vs. ~$39.6B consensus is the binary that will drive the reaction
ideas.edge: Stifel price target $1,500 (Buy), Wedbush $1,300 (Outperform), RBC $1,200 — all raised within the past week — vs. stock near ~$1,045 (pre-earnings), implying 14%-43% upside to recent targets; FY2026 EPS projected at $57.71, up 651% from FY2025's $7.68; consensus 'Strong Buy' from 27 analysts with zero sell ratings; options volatility has risen into the print
ideas.bull_case: HBM4 demand from hyperscalers remains insatiable, Micron guides Q4 above $40B and confirms HBM pricing holds, validating the re-rating from cyclical to structural AI growth stock — stock reprices toward $1,300-$1,500 analyst targets.
ideas.bear_case: SK Hynix supplied HBM4E samples to major customers on June 18, signaling intensifying competition; if Micron's HBM share trajectory or gross margin guidance disappoints, the stock — up ~300% YTD — could see a sharp post-earnings reversal. Rising capex ($20-25B in FY2026) and a potential Warsh-era rate hike compress multiples.

ideas.name: FedEx Corp.
ideas.thesis: FedEx reports Q4 FY2026 earnings on June 23 — the first quarterly print after the June 1 spin-off of its FedEx Freight division, resetting the financial profile of the company and giving investors the first clean look at the leaner, Express-centric 'new FDX.' Network 2.0 transformation savings are tracking above the $1B target for this fiscal year, and the company's own Q3 guidance implied Q4 adjusted EPS of ~$5.80-$5.92 as the highest quarter of the year. The critical focus will be on FY2027 guidance and the monetization strategy for the retained 19.9% stake in FedEx Freight.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings release June 23, 2026 after market close; first post-Freight-spin-off quarter — FY2027 guidance will be the primary market-moving variable, establishing the new baseline for a structurally simplified business
ideas.edge: Wall Street mean price target $385.32, median $420, high target $479 — per TIKR — vs. stock near 52-week highs; TIKR models FY2026 normalized EPS of $19.57 accelerating to $22.21 in FY2027 and $25.54 in FY2028 as Network 2.0 drives EBIT margins from 6.9% to 8.0%; 11 analysts revised earnings upward ahead of the print; the spin-off itself was called 'a value-unlocking event' by Raymond James
ideas.bull_case: FY2027 guidance comes in above consensus, management quantifies the Freight-stake monetization timeline, and Network 2.0 savings beat $1B — confirming the transformation thesis and giving the stock a path to the $420-$479 analyst target range.
ideas.bear_case: The Iran deal has lowered fuel surcharge revenue headwinds, but a potential October Fed rate hike would pressure volume growth and potentially soften guidance. One-time Freight spin-off costs and the grounding of the MD-11 fleet create noise in the headline EPS number that could spook near-term traders if not clearly explained.

ideas.name: Delta Air Lines, Inc.
ideas.thesis: The U.S.-Iran MOU and Strait of Hormuz reopening has sent WTI crude from ~$113/bbl at its April peak to ~$80 — a structural tailwind for airlines, whose fuel costs were tracking toward $350B industry-wide in 2026. Delta is the strongest fundamental name in the group: it has the best fuel-hedging profile among the major carriers, the strongest balance sheet, and broke to a new 52-week high following the deal. Morgan Stanley raised its target to $182 from $150, and Wells Fargo reiterated Buy in June. The Iran deal's 60-day negotiation window provides sustained de-escalation as a backdrop through summer travel peak.
ideas.catalyst: Strait of Hormuz reopening within 30 days per the June 19 MOU, directly reducing jet fuel costs during summer 2026 peak travel season; next earnings date (Q2 FY2026) typically falls mid-July, which would be the first quarter to fully reflect lower fuel costs
ideas.edge: Morgan Stanley raised price target to $182 from $150 (June 1) with a Buy rating; WTI has fallen ~30% from its April peak to ~$80, with jet fuel (a direct cost derivative) declining proportionally; IATA had estimated fuel costs consuming ~30% of industry expenses at peak oil — a $30/bbl reduction structurally restores multiple points of operating margin; DAL is up only ~19% YTD vs. UAL +3%, suggesting relative underperformance that could close
ideas.bull_case: Sustained $75-80 WTI oil through the summer (as Hormuz reopens) + strong summer demand = Q2 earnings beat and guidance raise in mid-July, pushing DAL toward the $182 MS target.
ideas.bear_case: The Iran MOU is a 60-day framework, not a permanent peace deal — if negotiations collapse or Iran slows Hormuz reopening, oil spikes back above $90-$100 and airline stocks retrace sharply. Additionally, a Fed rate hike in October could dampen consumer discretionary spending and future booking volumes.

ideas.name: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF
ideas.thesis: The Fed's hawkish pivot under Chair Warsh — removing easing bias, dot plot shifting to a median year-end rate of 3.8% (up from 3.4%), and markets now pricing a 25bp hike by October — has pushed 2-year Treasury yields to their highest level in over a year. The May PCE price index (due June 25) will be the next critical data point: officials have already upgraded their 2026 PCE forecast to 3.6% (from 2.7% in March), and a hotter-than-expected PCE print on Thursday could accelerate rate-hike pricing further and pressure long-duration bonds. TLT as a short-term hedge idea (put options or short outright) captures this risk into a confirmed near-term data catalyst.
ideas.catalyst: May PCE prices and Q1 GDP final estimate release on June 25, 2026 — a hot PCE print would validate the Fed's hawkish pivot and likely push 10-year yields higher, pressuring TLT; Fed bank stress test results also due June 24
ideas.edge: 2-year Treasury yields rose 0.16% on Fed day alone (June 17); the Fed now projects PCE at 3.6% for 2026 vs. 2.7% in March, a 90bp upward revision; May CPI already printed at 4.2% annualized — if May PCE is similarly elevated, markets may price in two hikes in 2026, which would be a material repricing event for long-duration bonds currently priced for a 3.8% terminal rate
ideas.bull_case: If May PCE comes in cooler than the Fed's upgraded forecast (suggesting the Iran-oil-shock inflation was transitory), rate-hike expectations could be pared back, providing a relief rally in TLT and long-duration assets broadly.
ideas.bear_case: For a short/hedge position in TLT: the Iran deal has already started to bring oil prices down, which could cause PCE to undershoot the Fed's 3.6% forecast and trigger a TLT rally — making this a time-sensitive trade that requires discipline on sizing and exits.
